description: A developer accumulating knowledge across an interconnected product portfolio over months. Tests cross-project recall, causal 'why did we decide' recall, temporal updates/supersession, and abstention.

memories.text: letX is a LaTeX-based resume and document builder. It consumes Quantum Sketch's video API to auto-generate tutorial walkthroughs for each template.
Quantum Sketch (QS) is splitting into two services: a web app (QS-2.0) for end users, and an API platform that other products call for programmatic video generation.
Context-Heavy is the shared brain. It exposes a REST API and an MCP server over SSE so external agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Hermes can share one memory.
common-knowledge is the local-first open-source skill. Its ck bridge pushes captured notes into Context-Heavy through POST /v1/sync/ck, so local notes land in the cloud brain.
Decision: Context-Heavy moved its embedding and chat provider from OpenRouter to NVIDIA NIM, because the OpenRouter key hit its total spending limit and started returning 403 errors.

Context-Heavy now embeds with BGE-M3 at 1024 dimensions. Migration 014 pinned the embedding column to vector(1024) and added an HNSW index, so semantic queries use an index scan instead of a full scan.
Context-Heavy's embeddings originally used a free multimodal model with an unconstrained vector column and no ANN index, so every semantic query was an exact full scan over all rows.
offSchool is an education product that uses both the Context-Heavy brain (for student memory) and Quantum Sketch's video API (for lesson videos).
aibhai is an experiment in the style of t3.chat — a fast multi-model chat and code playground.
Context-Heavy's Ask feature has three modes: fast (single-pass retrieval), deep (multi-hop, names what is missing and runs follow-up retrievals), and global (answers from community summaries).

questions.question: Which products consume Quantum Sketch's video API?
questions.answer: letX and offSchool both consume Quantum Sketch's video API.

questions.question: Why did Context-Heavy switch its embedding provider to NVIDIA NIM?
questions.answer: Because the OpenRouter key hit its total spending limit and returned 403 errors.

questions.question: What embedding model does Context-Heavy currently use, and at what dimension?
How do notes from common-knowledge get into Context-Heavy?
questions.answer: Through the ck bridge, which pushes notes via POST /v1/sync/ck.

questions.question: What price does offSchool charge enterprise customers per seat?
What does Context-Heavy expose so external agents can share its memory?
questions.answer: A REST API and an MCP server over SSE.

questions.question: Did Context-Heavy ever run without an ANN index on its embeddings?
questions.answer: Yes. Originally it used an unconstrained vector column with no ANN index and every query was a full scan, until migration 014 added the HNSW index.
